title: Modesciadium involucratum
intro: Modesciadium involucratum, also known as Sumbul or Indian Pennywort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Modesciadium involucratum include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Modesciadium involucratum is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are popular for their potential to promote relaxation, improve cognitive function, and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Modesciadium involucratum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a member of the tribe Anthemideae and the genus Modesciadium. The classification of Modesciadium involucratum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the rank of order, it belongs to the Apiales. Modesciadium involucratum is also known by the synonyms of Anthemis involucrata and Anthemis involucratum.
